question_text: Different food types store different amounts of chemical energy. Which of the following correctly ranks these macronutrients by their energy content per gram?

options.label: A
options.text: Carbohydrates (4 J/g) > Proteins (4 J/g) > Fats (9 J/g)

options.label: B
options.text: Fats (9 J/g) > Carbohydrates (4 J/g) = Proteins (4 J/g)

options.label: C
options.text: Proteins (9 J/g) > Fats (4 J/g) > Carbohydrates (4 J/g)

options.label: D
options.text: Carbohydrates (9 J/g) > Fats (9 J/g) > Proteins (4 J/g)

correct_answer: Fats (9 J/g) > Carbohydrates (4 J/g) = Proteins (4 J/g)
explanation: Different macronutrients store different amounts of chemical energy per gram. Fats are the most energy-dense, containing about 9 joules per gram, while carbohydrates and proteins each contain about 4 joules per gram. This is why fats are often used for long-term energy storage in living organisms.
